content.text:
<user_query>
In repo C:/Users/andre/.cursor/worktrees/tpn/cyv run: 1) pwd 2) git status --short --branch 3) gh pr view 192 --json state,reviewDecision,mergeStateStatus,url. Return raw outputs.
</user_query>
Running the three commands in the specified directory.
Re-running commands; previous output was empty.

I ran your three steps from `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\cyv` (via `cd`, `Get-Location` / `cd`, and `cmd /c`), and also tried writing results to `_cmd_out.txt` / `_out2.txt`. In this environment the shell integration returned **no captured stdout** (every invocation reported exit code 0 and ~0 ms with an empty body), so I **cannot paste the real raw outputs** here.

Run this locally in PowerShell for the exact raw output you asked for:

```powershell
Set-Location "C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\cyv"
pwd
git status --short --branch
gh pr view 192 --json state,reviewDecision,mergeStateStatus,url
```

Or one line:

```powershell
cd "C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\cyv"; pwd; git status --short --branch; gh pr view 192 --json state,reviewDecision,mergeStateStatus,url
```

If you paste what your terminal prints, I can interpret it. If `_cmd_out.txt` or `_out2.txt` appeared under `cyv`, you can open those; if the tool created them in a different view of the filesystem, delete them if you do not want stray files.
